spec_match.rationale: The demo directly covers the specified distinction between the initial none value, a positive normalized length, and zero's infinite scaling factor.

interactive.rationale: Three mode buttons and the dash slider drive the real SVG pathLength fallback or CSS property and update exact textual results.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The feature card promises a comparison of none, 1000, and zero semantics, and the page provides precisely that comparison.

edge_cases.rationale: The concept is dedicated to the important zero edge case and contrasts it against both the initial value and a normal positive value.

references.rationale: The page links ChromeStatus feature 4861677550043136, the current spec pull request, and the SVG WG zero-semantics discussion.

conformance.rationale: The feature conformance route reports no suite, so the crucial zero-versus-none behavior has no immutable browser assertion.

runtime_evidence.rationale: Chrome DevTools MCP in Headless Chrome 149 exercised none, 1000, zero, and the dash slider; zero set pathLength=0, retained a non-zero dash, showed the solid-state explanation, loaded resources, and produced no console errors.

telemetry_health.rationale: The shared telemetry client loaded and POSTed successfully, but the selected mode and dash interaction were not exposed as inspectable interaction events during review.

design_system.rationale: The page uses shared color variables, correctly pairs active button background and foreground, and centers the explanation on an interactive SVG stage.

accessibility.rationale: Buttons and slider are keyboard-focusable and named, but the selected mode has no aria-pressed state, the visible slider label is not associated, and changing explanation/readout content is not announced as live status.

openQuestions.title: Add a feature-level conformance suite
openQuestions.detail: Create exact assertions for path-length parsing and computed values, including the distinct initial none and explicit zero contracts and the non-zero dash rendering behavior described by the SVG specification and WPT.

openQuestions.title: Expose selected mode and changing results accessibly
openQuestions.detail: Treat the three mutually exclusive mode buttons as an announced stateful group (for example aria-pressed buttons), associate the visible dash label with the slider, and expose the changing verdict/readout through suitable status semantics.

summary: Chrome DevTools MCP tested http://localhost:3001/v150/support-path-length-as-a-css-property/zero-edge-case/ in Headless Chrome 149. The reviewer switched from none to 1000 to zero and changed dash from 300 to 100; the DOM reported pathLength=0, stroke dash 100px, and the infinite-scaling solid-stroke verdict. Unsupported CSS property handling was explicit and the SVG attribute fallback remained functional. Console was clean; all resources and the telemetry POST succeeded. The accessibility tree exposed named buttons and slider but no selected/pressed mode state. The feature conformance route reports no suite. Screenshot: /tmp/showcase-v150-zero-edge.png.
